Lumber Prices Have Tumbled This Month — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Dezember

Lumber futures have dropped about 12% since hitting a three-year high two weeks ago, a sign that wood buyers stocked up before duties on Canadian two-by-fours more than doubled this month and that traders are worried about the U.S. housing market.

Futures for September delivery fell to around $610 per thousand board feet late Friday and have declined in nine of the past 10 trading sessions. On-the-spot prices are also down, according to wood-pricing service and trade publication Random Lengths.

Canadian producers are charged antidumping and countervailing duties as a result of a decades-long trade dispute between the countries. The duties are separate from broad tariffs on lumber imports that President Trump has said he is considering in the name of national security.

"Canadian producers raised quotes in varying degrees to account for the countervailing and antidumping duties that now top 35% but found few customers willing to pay higher prices," Random Lengths said Friday in its weekly market report.

Jordan Rizzuto, chief investment officer at GammaRoad Capital Partners, uses price moves in lumber, copper and gold futures to inform the firm's investing strategy, which skews toward the S&P 500 stock index or Treasuries depending on the signals. He said that besides indicating that lumber was piled high in U.S. lumberyards before the higher duties took effect, the whipsaw in wood prices is a warning sign for other asset classes.

"Lumber's price behavior over the past several weeks relative to countercyclical and defensive assets suggests potential weakening of new construction and cyclical sectors of the economy," he said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Monogram Technologies Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Monogram Technologies Inc., a company specializing in advanced robotic systems for orthopedic surgeries, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business developments during this period.

Financial Highlights

Product Revenue: $0 million. The company did not have any product sales during the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, and 2024.

Gross Profit: $0 million. No gross profit was recorded as there were no product sales.

Loss from Operations: $(3.1) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, a decrease from $(3.6) million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to reduced operating expenses.

Net Loss: $(5.4) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase from $(3.5) million in the same period in 2024, driven by a $2.5 million increase in the expense accrual related to terminating the Mt. Sinai License Agreement.

Net Loss Per Share: $(0.16) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.11) in the same period in 2024, reflecting the increased net loss.

Business Highlights

New Product Launches: Monogram Technologies Inc. announced that the FDA granted 510(k) clearance for its Monogram mBôs™ TKA System on March 17, 2025. This clearance allows the company to market the device, marking a significant milestone in its product development.

Pending Merger: The company entered into a Merger Agreement with Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. on July 11, 2025. This merger will result in Monogram becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zimmer Biomet, subject to certain conditions.

Regulatory Update: The company received regulatory approval from India’s Central Drugs Standard Control Organization to conduct a 102-patient clinical investigation of its Monogram TKA System, in collaboration with Shalby Limited.

Geographical Performance: The company is focusing on initial placements of its FDA-cleared mBôs™ TKA System with key surgeon KOLs in strategic geographies to establish clinical experience and demonstrate the system’s advantages.

Future Outlook: Monogram plans to integrate recent upgrades to its cutting system and other enhancements into the cleared mBôs™ TKA System, aiming for strategic market adoption and positioning for new opportunities.

Macroeconomic and Geopolitical Environment: The company is monitoring potential impacts from new tariffs and geopolitical tensions, which could affect future operations, including costs for components and materials.

Operational Strategy: Monogram is committed to a disciplined, long-term commercialization strategy, focusing on strategic market adoption and leveraging its FDA-cleared robotic system to de-risk its platform and validate its technology.

data.title: Global Interactive Technologies, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:

Nucor Stock Gets a Boost From Buffett. Berkshire's Last Steel Buy Didn't Go So Well. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Berkshire Hathaway bought Nucor stock. Hopefully, this purchase works out better than the last time Warren Buffett dabbled in the steel business.

Berkshire purchases often boost stocks. Shares of the steel maker traded as high as $151.32 on Friday and were around $146.34, up 1.4%, in midday trading. The S&P 500 fell 0.3%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.1%.

Berkshire, of course, has an incredible investing pedigree. Buying the shares is a big vote of confidence in Nucor.

There is a lot to like about the company. It's one of the 20 largest steel makers in the world, growing for decades by pioneering the use of smaller electric arc furnaces, called mini-mills, to melt lower-cost scrap metal.

It's also an S&P 500 dividend aristocrat, one of the 69 companies in the index that have raised dividends annually for at least 25 years. Nucor stock yields about 1.5%.

Still, it's a steel company in a tough commodity business that occasionally gets whipsawed by federal policy. That, historically, has meant low valuation multiples and high earnings volatility.

Nucor shares trade for about 13 times estimated 2026 earnings, a big discount to the S&P 500 multiple of about 22 times. The company earned almost $29 a share in 2022, when the benchmark steel price averaged about $1,000 a ton. Nucor made about $8.50 a share in 2024, when steel prices averaged closer to $750 a ton.

Steel prices today are about $830 a ton, boosted by President Donald Trump's 50% import tariffs on steel and aluminum. Wall Street expects Nucor to generate 2025 earnings of about $8.30 a share. Steel prices only recently started to rise. What's more, some steel business is done on annual contracts. Prices embedded in contracts tend to lag spot prices for the commodity.

Low multiples are typically attractive to value investors like Warren Buffett. His Berkshire Hathaway bought shares of Korean steel leader Posco around 2o06, when the company's stock traded at about 7 times next year's earnings at the time.

Posco stock roughly tripled from 2006 into 2007 as steel markets tightened with China's construction boom in its early innings. Shares, however, gave up all the gains during the 2008-09 financial crisis. Overall, Berkshire's Posco investment turned out OK. Berkshire sold the stock around 2013 and, while exact numbers are hard to come by, it likely earned a single-digit annual return holding shares.

This time, Berkshire is buying at a cyclical low. Earnings are off prior peaks. That's a reasonable strategy for investing in cyclical industries. Still, predicting the path of the steel business has been notoriously hard for decades.

Other steel stocks weren't getting as big a bump as Nucor shares. Cleveland-Cliffs stock was 0.8% at $10.64 a share. Steel-Dynamics stock was 0.1% at $127.65.

Coming into Friday, Nucor has risen about 24% this year but has declined about 1% over the past 12 months.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

data.title: The Dow comes close, but once again, no cigar
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dow ends slightly higher, S&P 500, Nasdaq post modest losses
  • Financials weakest S&P sector; Healthcare leads gainers
  • Dollar down; bitcoin dips; crude down >1%; gold ~flat
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.32%

THE DOW COMES CLOSE, BUT ONCE AGAIN, NO CIGAR

The Dow Industrials hit a fresh record intraday high early on Friday, for the first time since December 4, 2024. This, as UnitedHealth's shares jumped after Berkshire Hathaway raised its stake in the health insurer, while investors also assessed mixed data to determine the Federal Reserve's next monetary policy move.

However, strength proved fleeting for the blue-chip average, and a reversal took hold. UNH still rose 12%, while contributing a gain of 200 index points to the DJI.

After hitting 45,203.52, the Dow closed up just 0.08% at 44,946.12. With this, it once again failed to set a new record close, ending down 0.15% from its 45,014.04 December 4, 2024 record finish.

The S&P 500 also hit a fresh record intraday high. However, the benchmark index closed down about 0.3%. The Nasdaq Composite lost 0.4%.

Markets are also focused on a meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin, with hopes it could pave the way for a resolution to the Ukraine conflict and determine the outlook for crude prices.

A majority of S&P 500 sectors ended lower with financials , down more than 1%, the weakest group. Healthcare , up more than 1.6%, with its biggest boost also from UNH, led gainers.

Communication services advanced, and ended at a record close.

Under the surface, big banks (.SPXBK), regional banks and chips were among weaker groups.

For the week, the Nasdaq rose 0.81%, the SPX gained 0.94%, and the Dow rallied 1.74%. It was the Dow's biggest weekly gain since early July.

data.title: US appeals court grants Argentina request to put YPF share turnover on hold
data.description:
A U.S. appeals court on Friday granted Argentina's request to put on temporary hold a judge's order that it turn over its 51% stake in oil and gas company YPF to partially satisfy a $16.1 billion judgment won by two investors.

The 2nd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in Manhattan stayed U.S. District Judge Loretta Preska's June 30 turnover order while Argentina appeals.

Argentina has warned that it would suffer irreparable harm and its economy could be destabilized if it gave up its stake in YPF, the country's largest energy company.

The appeals court did not provide reasons for its order.

Preska had awarded the $16.1 billion in September 2023 to Petersen Energia Inversora and Eton Park Capital Management.

They sued over Argentina's 2012 decision to seize the YPF stake from Spain's Repsol without making a tender offer to minority shareholders.

Lawyers for Petersen and Eton Park did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

A spokesperson for Argentina's government said it was confident the $16.1 billion judgment would be overturned in the appeal.

data.title: Tesla Stock Drops. This Sales Stream Is Drying Up. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Tesla stock was down in early trading Friday, after a report that a key source of income was fading away.

Shares of the electric vehicle maker were down 0.9% at $332.71, while the S&P 500 was down 0.1% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.1%.

The move came after The Wall Street Journal reported that Tesla peers Rivian and Lucid were having trouble getting required paperwork from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, or NHTSA, for finalizing zero-emission credit deals.

Car makers essentially earn credits for making zero-emission vehicles. Auto makers that don't make enough can buy credits from those that do. EV makers only sell zero-emission vehicles, so they have excess credits to sell.

Tesla has generated about $6.4 billion in credit sales over the past three years, which is about one-quarter of the company's reported operating income over that span. Rivian generated roughly $160 million in credit sales in the first half of 2025. It doesn't expect to generate any additional sales for the rest of the year. Management's initial expectation was for $300 million in credit sales for 2025.

Credit sales are on the decline after the Trump administration removed penalties for violating federal emissions standards and took away California's authority to regulate its air emissions.

Rivian stock was up 1.6% in early trading at $12.25. Lucid stock was flat at $2.25 a share.

The reason the moves aren't uniform, or larger, is that the loss of credit sales has been on investors' radar for months. The paperwork issue is an example of how the issue is unfolding.

NHTSA didn't immediately respond to Barron's request for comment about the credits.

"NHTSA is focusing on fixing CAFE standards to make cars more affordable again," the agency told the WSJ. "When that process is complete, we will return to issuing compliance letters to manufacturers."

Coming into Friday trading, Tesla stock was down about 17% year to date and up about 57% over the past 12 months. Shares were also up about 1.8% for the week.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

data.title: Deere's Earnings Brought a Surprise. It Wasn't the Usual Kind. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Deere earnings landed with a thud, with investors focused on an unexpected cut to the company's fiscal year 2025 net income guidance. How Wall Street, and the stock, reacted to the quarter was an even bigger surprise.

Thursday, Deere reported fiscal third-quarter earnings per share of $4.75, better than the $4.58 analysts were looking for. The problem was that the midpoint of management's range of forecasts for net income in fiscal 2025, the year through October, is now $5 billion. The prior range, provided in May, had a midpoint of $5.15 billion.

Investors don't like negative surprises in quarterly results, but this one didn't come out of the blue. Everyone knows that 2025 has been tough, and investors are betting on improvement in 2026.

"We see nothing in today's results that calls into question the thesis that 2025 should mark a bottom in the ag cycle," wrote Jefferies analyst Stephen Volkmann in a Thursday report. He rates shares Hold and has a $510 price target for Deere stock.

The stock added 2.1% on Friday, closing at $488.80, after falling 6.8% on Thursday. The S&P 500 dropped 0.3% on Friday, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 0.1%

"Gets worse before it gets better," wrote Bernstein analyst Chad Dillard in a Thursday report, adding that "downside risk" is priced into the stock. He rates shares Hold and has a $487 price target for the shares.

Only a few Wall Street analysts cut their target prices for the stock in response to the result. One firm raised its number.

Overall, the average price target among analysts tracked by FactSet ticked down to about $536 from closer to $549 at the end of the second quarter. Target prices were down roughly 2% over the past few weeks.

Analysts' continued confidence, and the fact that guidance for 2025 was reduced only marginally, raise the question of why the stock fell hard on Thursday. The answer is probably just that investors had expected too much. Coming into the earnings, Deere stock was up north of 20% for the year with a gain of more than 45% over the past 12 months.

And Deere peer AGCO had just reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings on July 31. Its shares jumped 11% in response. CEO Eric Hansotia said he expected demand to improve around the globe in 2026.

An improvement is still the expectation. Deere management said the company plans to produce in line with retail sales in 2026, which would mark an improvement.

In 2025, Deere has been producing fewer tractors and equipment than its dealers have been selling as it seeks to restore inventories to normal levels. Less production and smaller wholesale volumes mean higher per-unit costs and lower sales.

Wall Street has some doubts about the recovery. "With the outlook staying one of caution...it seems upside scenarios for fiscal year 2026 are reducing," wrote BMO analyst Joel Jackson in a Friday note. He rates shares Hold and has a $460 price target for the stock.

"Headwinds persist," wrote Baird analyst Mig Dobre on Thursday. He rates shares Hold and lowered his price target by 6% to $488.

Overall, 44% of analysts covering the company rate shares Buy, according to FactSet. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%.

Barron's wrote positively about Deere recently, believing 2025 was the bottom of this agricultural cycle and that Deere's investments in automation and AI would improve margins in the future. That article appeared in mid-July with the stock just over $500.

We still believe an improving backdrop that would help the stock is on the way. Predicting when that happens is as hard as predicting the weather.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

data.title: H&R BLOCK INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
H&R Block Inc., a leading provider of global tax preparation services, financial products, and small business solutions, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive tax preparation industry. The company operates primarily in the U.S., Canada, and Australia, offering services through company-owned and franchise offices, as well as online and mobile platforms.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $3,760.995 million, increased by 4.2% from the prior year, driven by higher U.S. assisted tax preparation and DIY tax preparation revenues.
  • Net Income from Continuing Operations: $609.450 million, increased by 1.9% from the prior year, reflecting improved operating performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $4.42, increased by 6.8% from the prior year, indicating strong earnings growth per share.
  • EBITDA from Continuing Operations: $976.343 million, increased by 1.4%, highlighting stable earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: H&R Block provides global tax preparation services, financial products, and small business solutions, blending digital innovation with human expertise.
  • Tax Preparation Services: The company offers both assisted and DIY tax preparation solutions, guaranteeing 100% accuracy for both assisted and DIY tax returns.
  • Small Business Solutions: Through Block Advisors and Wave, H&R Block supports small businesses with bookkeeping, payroll, advisory, and payment processing solutions.
  • Recent Developments: The company entered into a Fifth Amended and Restated Credit and Guarantee Agreement, extending its line of credit maturity to 2030. Curtis A. Campbell was appointed to succeed Jeffrey J. Jones II as President and CEO, effective December 31, 2025.
  • Human Capital: H&R Block employs approximately 4,300 regular full-time associates and relies on a seasonal workforce, peaking at around 70,100 employees during the fiscal year.
  • Service Marks and Trademarks: The company markets its services and products under registered trademarks, particularly the "H&R Block" brand.
  • Seasonality of Business: A substantial majority of H&R Block's revenues are earned from February through April, aligning with the tax filing season.
  • Competitive Conditions: H&R Block faces competition from tax preparation firms, software providers, accounting firms, and governmental organizations.
  • Regulatory Environment: The company is subject to various regulations, including tax preparer regulations, financial consumer protection, privacy regulations, and franchise regulations.
  • Future Outlook: H&R Block plans to continue expanding its digital and small business solutions, leveraging technology and human expertise to enhance client outcomes and operational efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: H&R Block has focused on expanding its tax preparation services through acquisitions of franchise offices, enhancing its small business solutions through Wave, and investing in technology-related expenses, including cloud-related technology.
  • Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through a $1.5 billion share repurchase program, with $400.1 million of shares repurchased in the fiscal year 2025. It also increased its quarterly dividends, paying out $197.3 million in dividends in 2025 compared to $179.8 million in 2024. The company maintains a $1.5 billion credit line, extended to July 2030, to support working capital and other corporate needs. Capital expenditures increased to $82.0 million, focusing on retail office improvements and technology investments.
  • Future Outlook: H&R Block plans to continue its share repurchase program with $1.1 billion remaining authorized. The company aims to maintain its dividend payments and explore further acquisitions to enhance its service offerings. The extension of the credit line to 2030 provides flexibility for future capital needs, and the company expects to leverage its liquidity options to support strategic growth initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Risks: H&R Block faces significant competition in the tax preparation industry from tax return preparation firms, software providers, accounting firms, and governmental organizations like the IRS.
  • Operational Risks: The company relies heavily on a seasonal workforce, presenting challenges in hiring, training, and supervising associates during peak tax season. It also needs to effectively manage fraud, cybersecurity threats, and reliance on key vendors.
  • Regulatory Risks: H&R Block is exposed to various regulatory risks, including changes in tax laws, financial services regulations, and privacy laws that could impact operations.
  • Financial Risks: The company faces financial risks related to liquidity and the ability to maintain dividend payments. It is also subject to market risks, including fluctuations in interest rates and foreign currency exchange rates.
  • Management Strategies: Management is focused on strategic growth through acquisitions and enhancing technological capabilities to address competitive pressures.

data.title: Legence Files for IPO — Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Blackstone-owned Legence has filed for an initial public offering, joining a growing pipeline of potential public market debuts as the IPO market heats up.

The number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have yet to be determined, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday.

The San Jose, Calif., company intends to list its common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol LGN.

Legence provides engineering, installation and maintenance services for mission-critical systems in buildings, specializing in HVAC, process piping, and other mechanical, electrical and plumbing systems.

The company was acquired by Blackstone in 2020, when it was known as Therma Holdings. At the time, it was a conglomerate of several local and regional service providers with a wide range of offerings. It has since sought to bring its functions under a single umbrella aimed at reducing carbon emissions, implementing renewables and lowering utility costs.

Legence produced revenue of $2.1 billion in 2024, and registered a net loss of $27.6 million. Roughly a third of its revenue came from new building projects, and the rest from retrofits, upgrades and maintenance for existing buildings.

From 2021 to 2024, Legence grew revenue at a compound annual growth rate of 16% when accounting for the effect of acquisitions made during that period.

The company focuses on growing sectors with technically demanding buildings, including technology, life sciences, healthcare and education. Its clients comprise more than 60% of the companies in the Nasdaq-100 Index, it said.

As of June 30, Legence had about 6,000 full-time employees across 70 offices.

The company said it intends to use the proceeds from the offering to repay outstanding borrowings under its term loan credit facility. It will use remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes.

Goldman Sachs and Jefferies are the offering's joint lead book-running managers.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: QVC Group, Inc. Announces Semi-Annual Interest Payment and Regular Additional Distribution on 3.75% Senior Exchangeable Debentures Due 2030
data.description:
WEST CHESTER, Pa., Aug. 15, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — QVC Group, Inc. ("QVC Group") (, QVCGP; ) today announced the payment of a semi-annual interest payment and Regular Additional Distribution to the holders as of August 1, 2025 of the 3.75% Senior Exchangeable Debentures due 2030 (the "Debentures") issued by its wholly-owned subsidiary, Liberty Interactive LLC ("LI LLC"). The semi-annual interest payment amount is $18.75 per $1,000 original principal amount of Debentures, and the amount of the Regular Additional Distribution is $0.4257 per $1,000 original principal amount of Debentures.

Under the Indenture for the Debentures, the original principal amount of the Debentures is adjusted in an amount equal to each Extraordinary Additional Distribution made to holders of the Debentures. Thereafter, the adjusted principal amount is further reduced on each successive semi-annual interest payment date to the extent necessary to cause the semi- annual interest payment to represent the payment of an annualized yield of 3.75% of the adjusted principal amount. This latter adjustment, to the extent it is made by reason of a particular Extraordinary Additional Distribution that results in an adjustment to the principal amount of the Debentures, takes effect on the second succeeding interest payment date after the payment of that Extraordinary Additional Distribution.

To date, there has been one Extraordinary Additional Distribution to holders of the Debentures. On August 7, 2013, LI LLC made an Extraordinary Additional Distribution of $46.1258 per $1,000 original principal amount of the Debentures arising from the merger transaction between Sprint Nextel Corporation and SoftBank Corp.

Adjustments to the principal amount of the Debentures do not affect the amount of the semi- annual interest payments received by holders of the Debentures, which will continue to be a rate equal to 3.75% per annum of the original principal amount of the Debentures. Below is a detail of the amount of the semi-annual interest payment being made on the Debentures, its allocation between payment of interest and repayment of principal and the revised adjusted principal amount of the Debentures resulting from such payment, per $1,000 original principal amount of the Debentures:

LI LLC is also making a Regular Additional Distribution to holders of the Debentures as of August 1, 2025 of $0.4257 per Debenture, which is attributable to the regular quarterly cash dividend paid by T-Mobile US, Inc. of $0.88 per share on March 13, 2025 and $0.88 per share on June 12, 2025. The payment of the Regular Additional Distribution will not result in a reduction to the adjusted principal amount of the Debentures.

The semi-annual interest payment and Regular Additional Distribution are expected to be made on August 15, 2025, to holders of record of the Debentures on August 1, 2025.

On April 1, 2020, T-Mobile US, Inc. completed its acquisition of Sprint Corporation ("TMUS/S Acquisition") for 0.10256 shares of T-Mobile US, Inc. for every share of Sprint Corporation. Following the TMUS/S Acquisition, the reference shares attributable to each $1,000 original principal amount of Debentures consist of 0.2419 shares of common stock of T-Mobile US, Inc. and 0.5746 shares of common stock of Lumen Technologies, Inc. .

About QVC Group, Inc.

QVC Group, Inc. is a Fortune 500 company comprised of six leading retail brands — QVC(R) , HSN(R) , Ballard Designs(R) , Frontgate(R) , Garnet Hill(R) and Grandin Road(R) (collectively, "QVC Group(SM) "). QVC Group(SM) is a live social shopping company that redefines the shopping experience through video-driven commerce on every screen, from smartphones and tablets to laptops and TVs. QVC Group reaches more than 200 million homes worldwide via 15 television channels, which are widely available on cable/satellite TV, free over-the-air TV, and FAST and digital livestreaming TV. QVC Group also reaches millions of customers via its QVC+ and HSN+ streaming experience, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, websites, mobile apps, social pages, print catalogs, and in-store destinations. QVC Group, Inc. also holds various minority interests.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1545 ET [Dow Jones]--Live cattle futures on the CME closed trading up 1.7% to $2.31775 a pound, returning to a new high after Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins gave a press conference in Austin, Texas, about New World Screwworm. The press conference resulted with new developments in how the USDA plans to address the disease, although didn't step up the strictness of the ban or provide details on re-opening the border for Mexican cattle. "Nothing dramatic for new news, just more details of the current plan," says Naomi Blohm of Total Farm Marketing in a note. Lean hog futures closed the day up 1.1% to 90.475 cents a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1514 ET - U.S. natural gas futures settle higher with short-sellers taking profits after yesterday's bounce off support, but little change in the weather outlook. National demand is likely to be strong for the next four days, while the five to 15-day outlook sees some cooling in the Great Lakes and Northeast, NatGasWeather.com says in a note. "A little hotter trends would help the bullish case," the forecaster says, adding that weekend forecasts, "could decide if $3 is again in play on Sep'25 contracts to start next week." Gas for September delivery settles up 2.6% at $2.916/mmBtu, but is down 2.5% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1512 ET - Crude futures return most of the previous day's gains as traders remain cautious ahead of the meeting between Presidents Trump and Putin about ending the Russia-Ukraine war. "Crude continues trading in a defensive pattern," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler says in a note. Most traders view the meeting as preliminary, he says, "with no real results coming forth today which means Russian petroleum exports will continue." While a cease-fire announcement would be a near-term negative for oil, the possibility of Trump threatening more tariff pressure against buyers of Russian oil in the event of a stalemate "is keeping a nervous trade to crude." WTI settles down 1.8% at $62.80 a barrel for a 1.7% loss on the week. Brent falls 1.5% to $65.85 a barrel, down 1.1% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1432 ET - ICE coffee futures are up 5.5% in afternoon trading to $3.361 a pound. Driving prices higher is a new frost near coffee-growing areas in Brazil, with the market unable to confirm what kind of losses the flowering coffee crop may have sustained. It's the highest coffee futures have traded at since June, according to data from FactSet. Even with a U.S. tariff on Brazilian goods interrupting the flow of arabica coffee out of country, coffee remains one of the best-performing soft commodities on the ICE this year, Ole Hansen of Saxo Bank says in a note. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1356 ET - Corn is leading the way higher for row crops, with the most-active contract up 1.7%, pushing futures back above the $4/bushel threshold. Short covering is supporting the uptick as well as some traders seeing futures as a bargain at current levels, says Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note. Suderman points to next week's Pro Farmer Crop Tour as the next big event traders are watching for, in hopes of better understanding the size and scope of this year's crop. Soybeans are up 1.4%, while wheat rises 0.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1333 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. edged up for a second consecutive week, with operators adding one rig to 412, Baker Hughes reports. That's 71 fewer than a year earlier. Despite the declining rig count, oil production reached a record 13.49 million barrels a day in May, according to the EIA. The agency said this week that it expects U.S. production to hit a record near 13.6 million b/d in December, before falling to 13.1 million b/d by 4Q26 as oil prices fall and producers accelerate the decrease in drilling and well completions. The latest EIA output estimates put average crude oil production at 13.3 million b/d for the four weeks through Aug. 8. Rigs directed at natural gas slipped by one this week to 122, or 24 more than a year ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1330 ET - The harvest of hard red winter wheat in the U.S. has surpassed 80%, while soft red winter wheat is 100% done, says U.S. Wheat Associates in a note. The group says that precipitation in wheat areas has slowed down the harvest progress, but the wheat leaving the fields appears mostly optimal. "Protein, moisture and test weights looking very good," says the group. CBOT wheat futures are up 0.4%, while corn rises 1.6% and soybeans climb 1.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1136 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper up 0.2% at $9,796.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.7% at $2,605.0 a ton. Base metals have been well-supported over the past month by fading tariff concerns and copper supply disruptions in Chile and Africa, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen says in a note. Copper has steadied with a stronger focus on fundamentals after July's volatility, which was driven by unpredictable U.S. trade policy. China's latest trade data has hinted at some stabilization in copper imports, improving demand hopes, Hansen says. Meanwhile, copper supply concerns have arisen due to the suspension of underground operations at Chile's El Teniente mine following fatalities, and an environmental disaster at a Chinese state-owned mine in Zambia, Hansen adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1124 ET - Gold futures inch higher but are lower on week, with prices trapped in a relatively narrow range overall. Futures are up 0.2% at $3,390.70 a troy ounce, but are on track to end the week nearly 3% lower. The precious metal surged a week prior after U.S. Customs and Border Protection said it would impose levies on gold, but all gains were wiped out when President Trump clarified that bullion wouldn't be subject to tariffs after all, FxPro's Alex Kuptsikevich says in a note. Still, further losses were limited as the U.S. dollar was pressured by accelerating inflation, Kuptsikevich says. Gold has now been trading around the 50-day moving average for the past eight weeks, and technical trading indicators are showing signs of fatigue, he adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1045 ET - Brazilian sugarcane industry association Unica says that the Brazilian harvest of sugarcane, along with the production of sugar-based ethanol, is down from this time last year. In a report, the group says sugarcane production in the central/south region of Brazil is off nearly 3% from this time last year, at 50.2 million metric tons. Sugar production is down 0.8%, and total ethanol production fell 11%, to 2.3 billion liters. Sugar futures on the ICE are down 0.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1036 ET - Livestock futures on the CME are mixed, with live cattle up 0.3% while lean hogs are down 0.1%. For cattle, futures are higher ahead of a press conference from Agricultural Secretary Brooke Rollins regarding New World Screwworm. "The trade will be plenty curious if there's a timeline for re-opening the Mexican border, and maybe more importantly any plan that could accelerate the volumes of cattle flows beyond the relatively small daily totals seen in normal times," says StoneX in a note. For hogs, downward pressure is coming from lower pork cutout prices reported by the USDA late yesterday. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1003 ET - A crop tour being performed by analysts with AgResource continues to find impressive crops heading into Illinois. "It even got better as we… crossed the river and got to western Illinois," says Ben Buckner of AgResource. Little to no issues with pollination have been found in any of the corn fields surveyed, he says, but the soybean fields are stealing the show. "They just look incredible so far," he says. But as Dan Basse, also of AgResource notes, these crops still need "finishing rains" in order to maintain their good health. CBOT grain futures are up, with corn rising 0.6%, soybeans climbing 0.7%, and wheat 0.6% higher. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Finward Bancorp Announces Dividend
data.description:
MUNSTER, Ind.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 15, 2025--

Finward Bancorp (the "Bancorp" or "Finward"), the holding company for Peoples Bank (the "Bank"), today announced that on August 15, 2025 the Board of Directors of Finward declared a dividend of $0.12 per share on Finward's common stock payable on September 12, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on August 29, 2025.

About Finward Bancorp

Finward Bancorp is a locally managed and independent financial holding company headquartered in Munster, Indiana, whose activities are primarily limited to holding the stock of Peoples Bank. Peoples Bank provides a wide range of personal, business, electronic and wealth management financial services from its 26 locations in Lake and Porter Counties in Northwest Indiana and the Chicagoland area.
